learning_outcomes.description: Identify key concepts in food perception.
Evaluate food products using discriminative, descriptive, and affective sensory evaluation methods.
Integrate and critique assessment results in a group environment and communicate the results in written reports.

assessments.task_description:
The Quiz will be based on Statistical Concepts for Sensory/Instrumental Data.

The quiz will be conducted in class, online via Blackboard during lecture hours. Students must bring their own laptop. You will have 30 minutes to complete the quiz, worth 20 marks, in a single session, and only one attempt will be permitted. The quiz will include problem-solving, fill-in-the-blank, and true-or-false questions. This quiz contributes 20% to your overall grade.

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

Students are to bring their own device for this test.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The online assessment is completed within Blackboard under the appropriate assessment title on your course Blackboard site.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.weighting: <dd>20% Sensory Evaluation Report is worth 10% and Physical Assessment Report is worth 10% for a total of 20% </dd>
assessments.due_date:
Sensory Report (7% Weight) + Peer Assessment (3% Weight) 9/09/2026 2:00 pm

Physical Assessment Report (7% Weight) + Peer Assessment (3% Weight) 16/09/2026 2:00 pm
assessments.task_description:
Sensory Evaluation Report (7%) + Peer Assessment (3%)

This is a group assessment completed in self-selected groups of four students.

Each group will critically analyse an assigned sensory evaluation case study and submit one group report via Turnitin. Students are expected to work collaboratively, with each member taking primary responsibility for one allocated section while contributing to the report as a whole.

The written report contributes 7% to the course grade. All group members will receive the same mark for the report.

Students must also complete a Peer Assessment (3%), which evaluates each member's contribution to the group work.

Detailed report instructions, formatting requirements, report template, marking rubric, and submission information are available in the Assessment folder on Blackboard.


Physical Assessment Report (7%) + Peer Assessment (3%)

This is a group assessment completed in the same self-selected groups of four students formed for the Sensory Evaluation Report.

Each group will evaluate the physical properties of the assigned product through instrumental analysis of colour, texture, viscosity, and microstructure, and submit one group report via Turnitin. Students are expected to work collaboratively, with each member taking primary responsibility for one allocated section while contributing to the report as a whole.

The written report contributes 7% to the course grade. All group members will receive the same mark for the report.

Students must also complete a Peer Assessment (3%), which evaluates each member's contribution to the group work.

Detailed report instructions, formatting requirements, report template, marking rubric, and submission information are available in the Assessment folder on Blackboard.


Group Work Information and Advice

https://my.uq.edu.au/information-and-services/student-support/study-skills-and-learning-advice/study-skills-and-learning-advice-overview/group-work

Practical Attendance and Absences

Practical classes are an essential component of this course and provide the hands-on experience required to support the concepts introduced in lectures. Practical sessions are conducted weekly and run for approximately 2.5 hours.

As practical activities form the basis of this assessment, attendance is expected. The School is unable to provide alternative practical sessions for students who miss their scheduled class.

If you are unable to attend a practical due to circumstances beyond your control (e.g., illness), you must:

  • submit a Practical Absence Request as soon as possible;
  • submit your request before or on the day of the missed practical;
  • provide the required supporting documentation.

Information about acceptable reasons and supporting documentation is available at:

https://my.uq.edu.au/information-and-services/manage-my-program/exams-and-assessment/applying-assessment-extension?p=1#1

Requests submitted without appropriate supporting documentation may not be approved.

If your absence affects your ability to complete an assessment by the due date, you must also apply separately for an Assessment Extension in accordance with University policy.

The Practical Absence Request link is available in the Learning Resources (Blackboard Original) or Course Resources (Blackboard Ultra) folder.

If you require further assistance, please contact the School: agriculture@enquire.uq.edu.au

Criteria and Marking

Refer to the marking rubric available on the Course Blackboard site.

Acknowledging and Referencing AI Use

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference and/or acknowledge any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference and/or acknowledge generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

The use of AI or MT must be referenced appropriately. Please see assessment guidelines in Blackboard for specific instructions. Students may also refer to the Library guides found under Additional Assessment Information (Acknowledging and Referencing AI Use).
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.weighting: <dd>20% Invidual Reflection- Sensory (10% Weight) + Physical Assessment  (10% Weight) </dd>
assessments.task_description:
PRACTICAL TASK: INDIVIDUAL ORAL REFLECTION – SENSORY EVALUATION (10%)

Following submission of the Group Sensory Evaluation Report, each student will complete an individual oral reflection during their scheduled practical class. This assessment evaluates your individual understanding of the sensory evaluation project and your ability to explain, justify, and critically reflect on the work completed by your group.

Students may be asked questions about any aspect of the report, including the selected sensory evaluation method, sensory evaluation session design, data analysis, methodological considerations, interpretation of findings, and the application of sensory evaluation to food product assessment.

The oral reflection will be conducted individually and will normally take 8–10 minutes. Follow-up questions may be asked to clarify or further explore your responses.

This assessment must be completed independently. The use of notes, generative Artificial Intelligence (AI), or Machine Translation (MT) tools during the oral assessment is not permitted.


PRACTICAL TASK: INDIVIDUAL ORAL REFLECTION – PHYSICAL ASSESSMENT (10%)

Following submission of the Group Physical Assessment Report, each student will complete an individual oral reflection during their scheduled practical class. This assessment evaluates your individual understanding of the physical assessment project and your ability to explain, justify, and critically reflect on the work completed by your group.

Students may be asked questions about any aspect of the report, including the experimental design, colour, texture, viscosity and microstructure assessments, instrument selection and calibration, data analysis, interpretation of results, methodological limitations, and the relationship between physical properties and product quality.

The oral reflection will be conducted individually and will normally take 8–10 minutes. Follow-up questions may be asked to clarify or further explore your responses.

This assessment must be completed independently. The use of notes, generative Artificial Intelligence (AI), or Machine Translation (MT) tools during the oral assessment is not permitted.

Practical Attendance and Absences

Practical classes are an essential component of this course and provide the hands-on experience required to support the concepts introduced in lectures. Practical sessions are conducted weekly and run for approximately 2.5 hours.

As practical activities form the basis of this assessment, attendance is expected. The School is unable to provide alternative practical sessions for students who miss their scheduled class.

If you are unable to attend a practical due to circumstances beyond your control (e.g., illness), you must:

  • submit a Practical Absence Request as soon as possible;
  • submit your request before or on the day of the missed practical;
  • provide the required supporting documentation.

Information about acceptable reasons and supporting documentation is available at:

https://my.uq.edu.au/information-and-services/manage-my-program/exams-and-assessment/applying-assessment-extension?p=1#1

Requests submitted without appropriate supporting documentation may not be approved.

If your absence affects your ability to complete an assessment by the due date, you must also apply separately for an Assessment Extension in accordance with University policy.

The Practical Absence Request link is available in the Learning Resources (Blackboard Original) or Course Resources (Blackboard Ultra) folder.

If you require further assistance, please contact the School: agriculture@enquire.uq.edu.au

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 2 To Week 11(03 Aug - 18 Oct)
learning_activities.topic:
Practicals (Physical Assessment)

In physical assessment practicals, students will use texture analyzers, colourimeters, and viscometers to measure the colour and texture of food products objectively. This hands-on experience will enhance their ability to accurately evaluate food's physical properties and improve their technical skills.

Please note that there are no practical sessions scheduled for Week 1. Before the commencement of practicals in Week 2, it is essential that you complete the online Occupational Health and Safety (OHS) training and thoroughly review the Practical Assessment Manual.

This hands-on experience will enhance their ability to accurately evaluate food's physical properties and improve their technical skills. Please note that there are no practical sessions scheduled for Week 1. Before the commencement of practicals in Week 2, it is essential that you complete the online Occupational Health and Safety (OHS) training and thoroughly review the Practical Assessment Manual.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 2 To Week 12(03 Aug - 25 Oct)
learning_activities.topic:
Practicals (Sensory Evaluation)

In the sensory practicals, students will gain practical experience with the three fundamental methods of food sensory evaluation: discriminative, descriptive, and affective. Each method assesses distinct aspects of sensory perception, essential for a thorough understanding of sensory science.
learning_activities.topic_description: Practicals (Sensory Evaluation) In the sensory practicals, students will gain practical experience with the three fundamental methods of food sensory evaluation: discriminative, descriptive, and affective. Each method assesses distinct aspects of sensory perception, essential for a thorough understanding of sensory science.
